RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. section 36(d). Trial is March 11, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is March 7, 2013. Plaintiff does not stipulate that summary motions may be filed on shortened notice and summary motions will be dictated by the Code of Civil Procedure. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than February 15, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on February 28, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 22, 2013. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 8, 2013. No later than December 13, 2012, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than December 13, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on January 8, 2013 at 1:30pm. in Department 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 and the date of this conference is March 11, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 503. All counsel attending the conference must have knowledge about the fact of the case, and be present with the representatives who have authority to settle. Any person(s) need to appear by phone must seek approval by the Court prior to the conference. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 regarding the submission of the confidential settlement conference statements. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
